name: Cal Tjader
abstract: Callen Radcliffe Tjader Jr. ( JAY-dər; July 16, 1925 – May 5, 1982) was an American Latin Jazz musician, often described as the most successful non-Latino Latin musician.

about_quotes.speaker: Vince Lateano
about_quotes.evidence: I had worked the festival a few times with Cal Tjader... With Cal Tjader. Yeah... I joined May ’78... four years... we went... we got there... we didn't play a note... he was a wonderful guy to work for
about_quotes.doc_title: Vince Lateano interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 8, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Monk Montgomery
about_quotes.evidence: He wanted me to, he needed a bass player and wanted to know would I join the band... You know it worked out.

about_quotes.speaker: Nick Brignola
about_quotes.evidence: I walked in and walked right up to Cal and said, “I’d like to sit in.” ... So I sat in and I wound up, he hired me. ... I think I played five or six of those jam session things that they did.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nick Brignola interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, July 28, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Appleyard
about_quotes.evidence: Cal Tjader was another one that inspired me, told me I should go to New York, it was Cal.
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Appleyard interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, October 4, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Red Norvo
about_quotes.evidence: But even when I had the trio with Cal and Mingus, we had that same problem.

about_quotes.speaker: Earl Watkins
about_quotes.evidence: Well Cal Tjader, he joined us for a while of course... And I’m just trying to think of who the heck — people would come sit in... And then for a while there was just Vernon and Cal and Richard Wyands and I.
about_quotes.doc_title: Earl Watkins interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Francisco, California, August 7, 2002
